message:
fix: sync dog persistent state in session Start/Stop, clean up orphaned sessions (gt-h0jiu)

Three bugs fixed:
1. Stop now sets dog state to idle after killing tmux session, preventing
   resource starvation from invisible dogs stuck in 'working' state.
2. Start now sets dog state to working after successful session creation,
   keeping persistent state in sync with runtime session state.
3. Verification error path in Start now kills the just-created session
   before returning, preventing orphaned tmux sessions.

SessionManager now accepts a *Manager parameter to perform state updates.
State sync is best-effort (logged warnings, not failures) since the session
lifecycle is the primary concern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
